mission.title: Install and Configure Kuberhealthy on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuberhealthy is a Kubernetes operator that runs synthetic checks as pods, allowing you to continuously verify that your applications and clusters are functioning as expected. It integrates seamlessly with Prometheus for monitoring and provides a simple JSON status page for easy access to check results.

steps.title: Install Kuberhealthy via Helm
steps.description:
Add the Kuberhealthy Helm chart and install:
```bash
helm repo add kuberhealthy https://kuberhealthy.github.io/kuberhealthy/helm-repos
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace kuberhealthy
helm install kuberhealthy kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy --version 104
```
Or install with plain YAML:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy/master/deploy/helm/kuberhealthy/crds/comcast.github.io_kuberhealthychecks.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy/master/deploy/helm/kuberhealthy/crds/comcast.github.io_kuberhealthystates.yaml
```

steps.title: Enable built-in health checks
steps.description:
Kuberhealthy comes with several built-in checks. Enable the deployment check:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy/master/cmd/deployment-check/deployment-check.yaml -n kuberhealthy
```

steps.title: Access the status page
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the Kuberhealthy status page:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kuberhealthy -n kuberhealthy 8080:80
```
Open http://localhost:8080 to view the JSON status page.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuberhealthy
kubectl get khcheck -n kuberhealthy
kubectl get khstate -n kuberhealthy
```

resolution.summary: Kuberhealthy is installed and running synthetic health checks on the cluster with a JSON status page and Prometheus metrics.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kuberhealthy
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall kuberhealthy -n kuberhealthy
kubectl delete namespace kuberhealthy
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kuberhealthy kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy
```

troubleshooting.title: Check pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=kuberhealthy -n kuberhealthy
```
Common cause: insufficient RBAC permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Checks not running
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the KuberhealthyCheck resource exists:
```bash
kubectl get khcheck -n kuberhealthy
kubectl describe khcheck <check-name> -n kuberhealthy
```

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus metrics not available
troubleshooting.description:
Kuberhealthy exposes metrics at `/metrics`. Ensure your Prometheus ServiceMonitor is configured:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kuberhealthy
curl http://kuberhealthy.kuberhealthy.svc:80/metrics
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have access to a Kubernetes cluster and the kubectl command-line tool installed and configured.
